details.error:
/bin/bash: line 1: play: command not found

Command not found

content.text:
The function call returned an error because the "play game" command is not a valid command in the system. The system is unable to find the command "play". 

To fix this, you need to specify the exact command to play a game. For example, if you want to play a game called "game.exe", the function call should be:

{"name": "exec", "parameters": {"command": "./game.exe"}} 

Please replace "game.exe" with the actual command to play your game. 

If you are trying to play a text-based game, you might need to use a different approach, such as using a loop to continuously ask for user input and then process it. 

Here is an example of how you can create a simple text-based game using the "exec" function:

{"name": "exec", "parameters": {"command": "python game.py"}} 

This will run a python script called "game.py" which can contain the game logic. 

Please note that the "exec" function can pose a security risk if you're planning to execute user-supplied input, because it can evaluate any Python expression, which makes it possible to access and modify system internals. 

In a real-world application, you would want to use a more secure approach, such as using a game engine or a framework that is designed for creating games. 

Also, the "exec" function is not suitable for all types of games, especially those that require a lot of user interaction or complex graphics. For such games, you would need to use a more specialized tool or framework. 

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any further questions.
